nudges.text: Your score is still 0.00, meaning the correctness check is failing. Before writing more Triton kernels, first create a simple `ModelNew` that just wraps/copies the reference `Model` implementation using standard PyTorch ops (no custom Triton kernels) to establish a passing baseline score. Once you have a non-zero score, you can incrementally replace parts with optimized Triton kernels. Also, check the full test harness output (especially the SCORE line and any error messages) to understand why correctness is failing.

You had a working baseline (score 60.0) at step 20 but your Triton kernel changes broke correctness. Revert to the pure PyTorch `ModelNew` wrapper that passed, then try `torch.compile(mode="default")` on the forward method as your first optimization attempt — it's much easier to get correct than hand-written Triton kernels and often yields good speedups.

Your `torch.compile` version scored 71.6 — that's a real, valid score. The test harness doesn't reject `torch.compile`; it only checks correctness and speedup.
